methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $16M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Housing & Construction (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 1 same-sector peers exist in Housing & Construction, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Cuby Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Inversion Space
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

private.name: Cobod
private.rationale: Same sector (Housing & Construction), same stage (Series A)

data.narrative:
Material Evolution is a Series A–stage frontier housing and construction company founded by Liz Gilligan and Sam Clark. The firm has raised $16M to date. The Housing & Construction sector lacks sufficient public comparables; four private peers (ICON, Cuby Technologies, Inversion Space, and Cobod) exist but none provide a sufficiently tight peer set at the same stage and valuation tier to support a robust median-based approach.

Our valuation relies on stage-progression methodology, which applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier typical of Series A advancement to the $16M total capital raised as a proxy baseline. This yields an implied valuation of $32M. This method is grounded in observed capital dynamics at this stage, though its reliability depends on Material Evolution's trajectory being consistent with sector norms. The peer-median approach was not applicable given insufficient same-stage and same-tier comparables in Housing & Construction; a broad-sector median would introduce uncontrolled variance.

Our analysis supports a valuation range of $27M (low) to $37M (high), centered on $32M. The median estimate carries moderate confidence given the single-method foundation and the nascent, heterogeneous nature of frontier construction technology. Key uncertainties include unit economics durability, capital intensity of scaling, and regulatory environment tightening. A refresh incorporating revenue traction or revised capital deployment plans would strengthen conviction.
